highlights: Built in 1049 during Ly Dynasty, stands on single stone pillar above lotus pond symbolizing purity
Located between Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and Museum; free entry, takes 10-20 minutes to visit
Peaceful spiritual atmosphere with gardens despite surrounding busy streets in heart of Hanoi

thingsToKnow.crowds: Popular tourist destination with steady visitor flow, especially during Tet holidays. Early morning recommended for quieter experience.
thingsToKnow.safety: Must pass through security checkpoint with metal detector and bag search. Enter only from west side entrance. Dress code enforced: cover shoulders and knees.
thingsToKnow.accessibility: A few steps and stairs to main shrine; toddlers must be carried as strollers cannot go up. Site is well-maintained and suitable for most visitors.
thingsToKnow.seasonal: Particularly atmospheric during Tet holidays when many locals come to pray for new year.

visitorTips: Visit early morning for quieter crowds and better lighting for photos; 7am opening time recommended
Enter via west side entrance; Google Maps may direct you incorrectly. One Pillar Pagoda stays open all day unlike nearby Mausoleum
Combine visit with Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and Museum nearby; wear walking shoes for exploring surrounding gardens

faq.question: Is there an entrance fee?
faq.answer: Free entry to One Pillar Pagoda itself. Security checkpoint fee of 40,000 VND also provides access to Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum area.

faq.question: What is the dress code?
faq.answer: Cover shoulders and knees. Females cannot show skin above knee or shoulders. Dress code is enforced by guards.

faq.question: How long does a visit take?
faq.answer: Most visitors spend 10-20 minutes. It is a small site but historically and spiritually significant. Best combined with nearby attractions.

sentiment.positive: Visitors consistently praise the pagoda's spiritual significance, peaceful atmosphere, unique architecture on single pillar, historical importance, and strategic location near other major Hanoi sites.
